danger_signals.visual_form: Heroic units and invading parties visible on dungeon map tiles
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to dungeon integrity; invasion in progress or imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Dungeon Heart visual damage/health bar deterioration
danger_signals.meaning: Critical danger state; loss condition approaching if heart destroyed

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy forces emerging from portals or dungeon entrances
danger_signals.meaning: Direct invasion path opened; immediate tactical threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual alert and notification when trap detects enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy presence detected in monitored area; response required

danger_signals.visual_form: Orange/red animated lava terrain; destructible wooden bridges burning
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger; creature vulnerability and infrastructure risk
Defensive mechanism active; enemy routing in progress

safety_signals.visual_form: Central dungeon structure with health indicator
safety_signals.meaning: Core dungeon control intact; player power source stable
Secured dungeon space under player control

safety_signals.visual_form: Reinforced wall visual variant adjacent to owned tiles
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive preparation; slows enemy digging progress

safety_signals.visual_form: Enchanted door variants with defensive visual effects
safety_signals.meaning: Active defense; doors engage hostile creatures

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple friendly creatures resting in designated rooms
safety_signals.meaning: Dungeon morale stable; creature force at capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: Treasury room with accumulated resource display
safety_signals.meaning: Economic stability; capacity for construction and creature upkeep

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dungeon Heart emblem; the Hand cursor
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Heroic units and good creatures (Kingdom of Harmonia forces) display opposed aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Underground dungeon spaces; corrupted terrain; evil creature types (Bile Demons, Warlocks, Salamanders, Skeletons, Vampires)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Portal Gems as faction control markers; 'Lord of the Land' guardian emblems
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Heroic units appear as invading parties; contrast with evil creature designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Surface realm access points; Portal Gem guardian locations; defended territories above ground

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Horned Reaper creature design; evil warrior aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Story progression markers; realm conquest objectives tied to Portal Gem recovery

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map shows remaining unsecured realms; Portal Gem count/display updates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creature availability increases; new unit types become recruitable based on dungeon composition and research

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy units visible on dungeon tiles; breach points highlighted; multiple hostile creature types present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creatures automatically defend; alarm traps trigger; fear traps activate to route enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Imps actively digging; claimed tiles spreading across map; new rooms under construction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Imps prioritize digging and fortification; minimal combat engagement; creatures train in Combat Pit

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Treasury room visually full; gold display prominent; construction available without resource warning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can afford creature upkeep and trap/door construction; casino operational for creature exploitation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Treasury empty or minimal visual representation; gold seams exhausted in visible areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creature upkeep at risk; construction halted; imps forced to prioritize gem mining over expansion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Creatures in Lair show rest state; damaged creatures display visual health reduction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rested creatures work harder; damaged creatures reduced in combat effectiveness until healed; torture chamber converts enemy units

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Animated lava tiles destroy Wooden Bridges; Stone Bridges remain intact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creatures avoid lava terrain; pathfinding routes around hazards; fire damage reduces creature health

environmental_storytelling.example: Placement of rooms (Lair, Hatchery, Training Room, Library, Treasury) tells story of dungeon evolution from basic survival to military/research sophistication
Location of 'Lords of the Land' and defended Portal Gems across twenty realms visually conveys Kingdom of Harmonia's defensive infrastructure and power distribution
Mix of creature types (Bile Demons, Warlocks, Salamanders, Skeletons, Vampires) available tells story of dungeon specialization and alignment with evil forces
Progression from unfortified walls → reinforced walls → magical doors → traps shows defensive maturation and threat response
Wooden vs. Stone Bridge construction choice indicates environmental awareness and response to lava hazards in dungeon regions
Creature-versus-creature battles in specialized arena show dungeon's military culture and creature development beyond passive training room

environmental_storytelling.technique: Casino and torture chamber moral markers
environmental_storytelling.example: Presence of gambling facilities and torture chambers visually reinforce evil faction identity and creature subjugation methods

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Actively dig, claim tiles, fortify walls; respond to hand direction for labor tasks
Abandon digging to flee or seek dungeon heart protection; prioritize survival over resource gathering
Elevated work productivity and combat readiness; respond faster to threat detection
Reduced combat effectiveness; seek Hatchery for feeding; avoid combat if possible
Engage in skill development up to experience level four; accumulate combat experience
Engaged in creature-versus-creature battles; gain experience levels five and beyond through combat
Assault dungeon heart; destroy rooms and creatures; attempt to clear dungeon tiles
Flee affected tile areas; show visual panic state; retreat from dungeon
Regenerate mana passively; produce Imps; detect and send creatures to investigate threats
Visual damage indication; accelerated threat investigation; creature emergency response
Convert to friendly units via torture chamber; change faction visual markers and behavior

notes: Dungeon Keeper 2 employs a rich visual language built on spatial/territorial control, creature composition, and defensive infrastructure to communicate game state. The 3D isometric dungeon perspective allows players to 'read' dungeon health through room placement, creature density, and fortification progression. Faction identity is primarily communicated through creature aesthetics (evil vs. heroic), with the Kingdom of Harmonia represented as invading force rather than visible faction spaces. Portal Gems serve as both plot device and visual world-state markers showing campaign progress. The game's danger/safety signals rely heavily on visual density (creature concentration), active animation (trap triggers, combat), and structural integrity (heart damage, breached walls). Environmental storytelling is implicit in room progression and creature specialization rather than explicit narrative markers.
